rulings.Case Title: LESLIE BLACK VS. DOYLE MORSE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 9, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF LESLIE BLACK'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on June 9, 2022 Plaintiff to file a proof of service for her declaration filed on May 31, 2022. A copy of the proof of service must be provided to all parties/counsel and to the Court no later than 9:15 a.m. on June 9, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SOLOMON VARON ET AL VS. STANDARD CONSTRUCTION OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party On Petition For Release Of Property From Mechanics Lien (Notice)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 9, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS AS PREVAILING PARTY ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM MECHANICS LIEN is GRANTED, in part. No opposition filed. Petitioner is awarded $7,000 in attorneys' fees as the prevailing party under CCP Section 8488(c). The Court finds the amount of time counsel declares to have spent on certain tasks unreasonable and/or could have been done by a paralegal. These include: (1) the amount of attorney time declared to "coordinate service" over the course of 13 days between 12/15/21 and 12/28/21; (2) the amount of time declared to "finalize and file" a Notice of Hearing and proposed order on 1/28/22; (3) the amount of time declared to draft the ex parte application on 2/9/22; (4) the amount of time declared to prepare for the ex parte hearing. Petitioners are awarded $507 in costs pursuant to CCP Sections 1032 and 1033.5. Petitioners have not established the additional costs associated with the filing fees (e.g., the $225 fee to file the petition versus Petitioner's requested amount of $279). Counsel must provide evidence of the fees charged by their electronic filing service provider for the electronic filing or service of their pleadings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
